# vis_table { #cdmconnector.vis_table }

```python
vis_table(
    result,
    estimate_name=None,
    header=None,
    group_column=None,
    rename=None,
    type=None,
    hide=None,
    style=None,
    options=None,
)
```

Format a table (summarised_result-like DataFrame) into a display table.

## Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

| Name          | Type                                | Description                                                                        | Default    |
|---------------|-------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------|
| result        | DataFrame                           | Must contain estimate_value; estimate_name, estimate_type optional for formatting. | _required_ |
| estimate_name | dict                                | Display name -> format template, e.g. {"N (%)": "<count> (<percentage>)"}.         | `None`     |
| header        | list of str                         | Columns to use as header (pivoted into columns).                                   | `None`     |
| group_column  | list of str                         | Columns to group by (e.g. strata_level).                                           | `None`     |
| rename        | dict                                | Column renames for display (e.g. {"cdm_name": "Database name"}).                   | `None`     |
| type          | \'dataframe\' \| \'html\' \| \'gt\' |                                                                                    | `None`     |
| hide          | list of str                         | Columns to drop.                                                                   | `None`     |
| style         | str                                 | Style name (currently only affects future backends).                               | `None`     |
| options       | dict                                | Override default_table_options().                                                  | `None`     |

## Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

| Name   | Type                                                              | Description   |
|--------|-------------------------------------------------------------------|---------------|
|        | DataFrame, HTML str, or great_tables GT object (when type=\'gt\') |               |
